question.text: What is the minimum net worth requirement for a Category I Merchant Banker as per SEBI (Merchant Bankers) Regulations, 1992?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 7 of the SEBI Merchant Banking Regulations specifies that Category I Merchant Bankers shall have a net worth of not less than Rs. 50 crore. They must also generate Rs. 25 crore in revenue over the last three financial years. **The Trap:** Rs. 10 crore is the net worth for Category II Merchant Bankers, not Category I. Rs. 5 crore is the net worth for portfolio managers. **Cross-Exam Context:** Net worth requirements across intermediaries are heavily tested. Category I MB (Rs. 50 crore), Category II MB (Rs. 10 crore), Portfolio Manager (Rs. 5 crore), Stockbroker for MTF (Rs. 3 crore). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both categories must maintain 25% of the minimum net worth as liquid assets. MBs handling only debt/hybrid issues are exempt from revenue requirements.
